properties.remark: DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OF A GARAGE FROM A SMALL TORNADO REPORTED TO LAW ENFORCMENT BY HOME OWNER WHO WITNESSED THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDING FROM A SMALL CONVECTIVE SHOWER.
DIME SIZED HAIL COVERED THE GROUND 3 INCHES DEEP WITH SOME HAIL STONES AS LARGE AS QUARTERS.
HAILSTONES VARIED FROM NICKEL TO QUARTER-SIZED AND FELL CONTINUOUSLY FOR NEARLY 30 MINUTES ALONG STATE ROAD 9 AND BEAR CREEK ROAD BETWEEN DEARY AND HARVARD.
MEASURED 1.6 INCHES OF RAIN IN 20 MINUTES NEAR ALPOWA SUMMIT ALONG HIGHWAY 12. SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED JUST SOUTHWEST OF THE SUMMIT.
FLOODING REPORTED ON OLD ALBION ROAD.
FLOODING REPORTED ON ESTES ROAD.

DROVE FROM MT SPOKANE HIGH SCHOOL TO BEAR CREEK LODGE AND WITNESSED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL. SOME DENTS TO HIS VEHICLE. ALSO NOTICED WATER AND MUD FLOWING OVER THE ROAD IN LOW-LYING AREAS FROM ARGONNE ROUND-A-BOUT TO THE LODGE.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL...WIND GUSTS 30-40 KT. HAIL COVERED THE GROUND.

DELAYED REPORT FROM 6/30 AT A RANCH NEAR HOOPER, WA. THUNDERSTORM WINDS TOOK AN ENTIRE WALL OFF A 6 YEAR OLD BARN. DAMAGE EXTENDED AT LEAST 3 TRESTLES INTO THE BARN AND STRIPPED ALUMINUM SIDING AND SHINGLES FROM THE BARN. OTHER DAMAGE ON THE RANCH INCLUDED 6X6 POSTS BROKEN IN HALF AND THE TOP OF A TREE ESTIMATED TO BE 5 INCHES IN DIAMTER WAS COMPLETELY SHEARED OFF. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED 60-70 MPH.
8 INCH TREE SNAPPED OFF AND FELL INTO POWER LINES KNOCKING OUT THE POWER.
TREES BLOWN DOWN NEAR HOUSE.
TREE LIMBS BLOWN DOWN AND BLOCKING ELK-CHATTAROY ROAD.
TREE LIMB FALLEN AND BLOCKING PORTION OF ELK CHATTAROY ROAD.
AT STEAMBOAT ROCK STATE PARK. ONE TREE BLOWN OVER...LEANING ON A TENT. SEVERAL MORE TREES HAD 5 INCH LIMBS BROKEN.
LOCATED AT THE SUN BANKS LAKE RESORT...ON THE NORTH END OF BANKS LAKE.
LOCATED AT THE SUN BANKS LAKE RESORT...ON THE NORTH SIDE OF BANKS LAKE.
ALSO SAW A WIND GUST TO 44 MPH AND 1.00 OF RAIN IN ONE HOUR.
TREES DOWN NEAR MT. SPOKANE
3 TO 5 MINUTES OF NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL DAMAGED FIRE TRUCK.
HAIL IS COVERING THE GROUND. LOST RIVER AREA.
WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED 50-60 MPH.
WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED 50-60 MPH.
HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS AND WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 70 MPH. LOST POWER THREE TIMES. REPORT WAS FROM US/CANADA BORDER PATROL.
CHELAN COUNTY SHERRIFF REPORTED WATER OVER THE WHITE RIVER ROAD 2 1/2 MILES WEST OF LAKE WENATCHEE
WATER OVER HIGHWAY 2 NEAR BERNE. REPORTED BY CHELAN COUNTY SHERRIFF.
DELAYED REPORT FROM AUGUST 8TH.

DELAYED REPORT FROM AUGUST 8TH. NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL LASTED FOR 20 MINUTES AND RESULTED IN EXTENSIVE CROP DAMAGE. 300 ACRES OF WHEAT DESTROYED AS WELL AS 30 PERCENT OF BARLEY CROP. SEVERAL 4 INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS SNAPPED. MUD SLIDE THREE FEET DEEP. 1.46 INCHES OF RAIN IN 30 MINUTES.
TOP 40 FEET OF A 14 INCH DIAMETER TREE TOPPLED
A 50 FOOT...12 INCH DIAMETER PINETREE WAS DOWNED BY WIND.
HIGHWAY 20 JUST WEST OF TWISP CLOSED FROM 150 PM PDT TO 430 PM DUE TO SMALL MUDSLIDES ACROSS THE ROAD IN 4 PLACES.
ACCUMULATED UP TO 1 INCH DEEP
